expectations.text: [phase_contract][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: Tavily-first then Confluence fallback ordering
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly names the case focus and discusses the required ordering: "Case focus: Tavily-first then Confluence fallback ordering" and "Phase 3 work: 'spawn Tavily-first deep-research requests... '" plus "report-editor deep research must run tavily-search first and use Confluence only as a recorded fallback" and a dedicated section "Phase 3 'Tavily-first then Confluence fallback' Ordering — Demonstrability".

expectations.text: [phase_contract][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase phase3
expectations.evidence: result.md is centered on Phase 3 contract and artifacts: it states "Primary phase under test: Phase 3", details "Phase 3 entry: scripts/phase3.sh", lists Phase 3 required outputs (e.g., "phase3_spawn_manifest.json", "context/coverage_ledger_<feature-id>.md", artifact lookup), and the verdict is framed as "Benchmark Verdict (Blocking)" due to missing "Phase 3 runtime artifacts".
